behaviors.behavior: campaignSpecCandidates rebuilds a campaign sub-iterate's spec path from the never-evicted work_completed fields (campaign + sub_iterate_id) instead of the evictable agent-doc spec hint
behaviors.evidence: spec-candidates.test.ts::'rebuilds the `<id>-<slug>.md` path that the known layout misses' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: the sub-iterate id matches case-insensitively (run_id lowercases what the filename capitalises, e.g. run …-w1-… vs file W1-…)
an exact `<id>.md` outranks a `<id>-<slug>.md` prefix match, and the emitted order is deterministic
behaviors.evidence: spec-candidates.test.ts::'prefers an exact `<id>.md` over a `<id>-…` prefix match' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: the prefix match is bounded by the trailing '-' so sub-iterate W1 never claims W10's document
behaviors.evidence: spec-candidates.test.ts::'does NOT match a different id that merely shares a prefix (W1 must not eat W10)' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: non-markdown entries, sub-directories and symlinks are never offered as candidates (defence-in-depth ahead of the caller's realPathGuard)
behaviors.evidence: spec-candidates.test.ts::'ignores non-markdown entries and sub-directories' + 'does not offer a SYMLINK as a candidate' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: a missing/unreadable campaign directory yields [] rather than throwing (ENOENT/ENOTDIR/EACCES)
behaviors.evidence: spec-candidates.test.ts::'returns nothing when the campaign directory does not exist (no throw)' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: campaign slug and sub-iterate id from the event log are held to the strict id grammar, so neither can inject a path segment or traverse
behaviors.evidence: spec-candidates.test.ts::'REJECTS traversal / unsafe segments in either event-supplied field' (10 vectors) + resolver.eviction.test.ts::'ignores campaign fields that fail the id grammar' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: END-TO-END: a campaign sub-iterate's Spec artifact resolves through the resolver with NO iterates/<run_id>.json present at all (AC1 — the eviction steady state)
behaviors.evidence: resolver.eviction.test.ts::'resolves a campaign sub-iterate's Spec from the event log with NO agent-doc present' PASSED; DIFFERENTIALLY VERIFIED — removing the campaignSpecCandidates append makes exactly this case FAIL while the other 3 still pass

behaviors.behavior: END-TO-END: the Requirement artifact resolves its FRs from the event log with NO agent-doc present (AC2 — the triage item's literal ask; measured as already-correct, previously UNPINNED)
behaviors.evidence: resolver.eviction.test.ts::'resolves the Requirement's FRs from the event log with NO agent-doc present' PASSED (asserts the specific FR the event recorded, not merely non-empty)

behaviors.behavior: no Spec is invented when the campaign document is genuinely absent — honest absence is preserved, not replaced by a pointer to a missing file
behaviors.evidence: resolver.eviction.test.ts::'does NOT invent a Spec when the campaign document is genuinely gone' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: the resolved Spec is titled/fingerprinted by the candidate that actually matched (via resolveFirstDoc's returned index), including the appended campaign candidate at index 4+, and the pre-existing preference order is unchanged by the append
behaviors.evidence: resolver.candidate-pairing.test.ts (3 cases) PASSED — mid-list known-layout candidate, appended campaign candidate, and known-layout-wins-over-campaign when both exist

behaviors.behavior: moving the work_completed lookup ahead of the resolver cache check, and splitting the spec-path builders into spec-candidates.ts, leave every other Mission behaviour unchanged
behaviors.evidence: full server suite 241 files / 2821 passed, 1 skipped, exit 0 — including all 29 mission-context suites (347) and the mission-context route contract tests

surface_verification.justification: Server-internal read-path change with no API, schema, message-contract or client/** edit: the Mission context response shape is byte-identical, and the only difference is that a campaign sub-iterate's Spec artifact now RESOLVES in the case where it previously reported absent because iterates/<run_id>.json had aged out of its 50-entry retention window. The composition point is exercised directly rather than mocked — resolver.eviction.test.ts and resolver.candidate-pairing.test.ts drive the real resolveMissionContext over real temp-dir fixtures with no agent-doc present, which is the exact production steady state (measured: 214 iterate runs in this repo's event log vs 54 surviving agent-docs, 75% already evicted). The AC1 case is differentially verified: reverting the one-line append makes it, and only it, fail. No UI affordance, route, or rendering path changed, so there is no browser surface to exercise; the full server suite (2821 passed) covers the route consumer chain.
